title: DevPulse Analytics
description: Developer productivity dashboard aggregating GitHub, Jira, and CI metrics into actionable insights.
overview: DevPulse gives engineering managers a single pane of glass for team health — cycle time, PR throughput, incident frequency, and sprint predictability — without the noise of raw Jira exports.
challenge: Data lived across GitHub, Jira, PagerDuty, and CircleCI with no common schema. Managers spent hours in spreadsheets. The product needed to feel lightweight despite aggregating millions of events.
solution: I built an ETL pipeline in Node.js that normalizes events into a PostgreSQL star schema, with materialized views for dashboard queries under 200ms. The Next.js frontend uses Recharts for interactive drill-downs and server components for fast initial loads.
results: Onboarded 8 engineering teams in Q1. Average dashboard load under 1.2s. Identified a 30% bottleneck in PR review time that led to process changes saving 6 hours/week per team.
